vulnerability.title: Reconstructing an Akira Ransomware Kill Chain from Perimeter and Endpoint Logs, (Wed, May 27th)
vulnerability.summary: Most Akira write-ups focus on the ransom note or the encryption routine. By the time those show up the interesting forensic work is over. The questions that matter to defenders sit earlier. How did they get in. When did they get domain admin. What did they touch before the binary fired. Those answers live in the days before impact. They sit in two log sources that almost never get joined. The perimeter firewall and the Windows event channel.
vulnerability.title_ko: 경계 및 엔드포인트 로그에서 Akira 랜섬웨어 킬 체인 재구성(5월 27일 수요일)
vulnerability.summary_ko: 대부분의 Akira 글은 몸값 메모나 암호화 루틴에 중점을 둡니다. 그것들이 나타날 때쯤에는 흥미로운 법의학 작업이 끝났습니다. 수비수에게 중요한 질문은 더 일찍 시작됩니다. 그들은 어떻게 들어왔습니까? 언제 도메인 관리자를 얻었습니까? 바이너리가 발사되기 전에 그들은 무엇을 만졌습니까? 그 대답은 충격이 가해지기 며칠 전에 살아있습니다. 그들은 거의 결합되지 않는 두 개의 로그 소스에 있습니다. 경계 방화벽 및 Windows 이벤트 채널.
vulnerability.summary_3lines:
1. 수비수에게 중요한 질문은 더 일찍 시작됩니다.
2. 그 대답은 충격이 가해지기 며칠 전에 살아있습니다.
3. 그들은 거의 결합되지 않는 두 개의 로그 소스에 있습니다.

vulnerability.title: 7-Zip 제품 보안 업데이트 권고
CISA Adds Three Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
vulnerability.summary:
CISA has added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.

CVE-2026-8398 Daemon Tools Lite Embedded Malicious Code Vulnerability
CVE-2026-45321 TanStack Unspecified Vulnerability
CVE-2026-48027 Nx Console Embedded Malicious Code Vulnerability

These types of vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise.
Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise. BOD 22-01 requires Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to remediate identified vulnerabilities by the due date to protect FCEB networks against active threats. See the BOD 22-01 Fact Sheet for more information.
Although BOD 22-01 only applies to FCEB agencies, CISA strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure to cyberattacks by prioritizing timely remediation of KEV Catalog vulnerabilities as part of their vulnerability management practice. CISA will continue to add vulnerabilities to the catalog that meet the specified criteria.
vulnerability.title_ko: CISA, 알려진 악용 취약점 세 가지를 카탈로그에 추가
vulnerability.summary_ko:
CISA는 현재 악용되고 있는 증거를 바탕으로 알려진 악용 취약점(KEV) 카탈로그에 세 가지 새로운 취약점을 추가했습니다.

CVE-2026-8398 Daemon Tools Lite 내장 악성 코드 취약점
CVE-2026-45321 TanStack 지정되지 않은 취약점
CVE-2026-48027 Nx 콘솔 내장 악성 코드 취약점

이러한 유형의 취약점은 악의적인 사이버 행위자에 대한 빈번한 공격 벡터이며 연방 기업에 심각한 위험을 초래합니다.
구속력 있는 운영 지침(BOD) 22-01: 알려진 악용된 취약점의 심각한 위험 감소를 통해 KEV 카탈로그는 연방 기업에 심각한 위험을 초래하는 알려진 CVE(공통 취약점 및 노출)의 살아있는 목록으로 확립되었습니다. BOD 22-01은 FCEB(연방민간행정부) 기관이 ​​식별된 취약점을 기한까지 수정하여 활성 위협으로부터 FCEB 네트워크를 보호하도록 요구합니다. 자세한 내용은 이사회 22-01 사실 자료를 참조하세요.
BOD 22-01은 FCEB 기관에만 적용되지만 CISA는 취약점 관리 관행의 일환으로 KEV 카탈로그 취약점을 적시에 해결하는 데 우선순위를 두어 모든 조직이 사이버 공격에 대한 노출을 줄일 것을 강력히 촉구합니다. CISA는 지정된 기준을 충족하는 취약점을 카탈로그에 계속 추가할 예정입니다.

3. BOD 22-01은 FCEB(연방민간행정부) 기관이 ​​식별된 취약점을 기한까지 수정하여

vulnerability.title: Eppendorf BioFlo 320
vulnerability.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to gain full access to functionality and data with the bioreactor.
The following versions of Eppendorf BioFlo 320 are affected:

BioFlo 320 Bioreactor vers:all/*





CVSS
Vendor
Equipment
Vulnerabilities




v3 9.8
Eppendorf
Eppendorf BioFlo 320
Use of Hard-coded Password




Background

Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Healthcare and Public Health
Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide
Company Headquarters Location: Germany


Vulnerabilities

Expand All +

CVE-2026-7251

The affected product is vulnerable to due to VNC server using a hard-coded password. If a remote attacker knows the network address of any BioFlo 320 model with remote access enabled, they can gain full control of the user interface by using this password. Once connected, the attacker would have full access to all control panel features for the BioFlo 320. VNC traffic is not encrypted.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
Eppendorf BioFlo 320

Vendor:Eppendorf
Product Version:Eppendorf BioFlo 320 Bioreactor: vers:all/*
Product Status:known_affected


Remediations
MitigationEppendorf has released a software update that permanently removes VNC access from the controller. Users should download and apply this update from: https://www.eppendorf.com/software-downloads.https://www.eppendorf.com/software-downloads
MitigationAll affected BioFlo 320 systems always shipped with Virtual Network Computing (VNC) disabled by default, and VNC can only be enabled locally at the tower. Eppendorf has removed VNC configuration information from all current documentation, so it no longer appears in BioFlo 320 Operating Manuals.
MitigationEppendorf recommends user do the following:Verify that VNC is disabled on the controllerEnable security so that only Admin and Supervisor roles can change VNC settingsInstall Version 5.0 Software as soon as possible

Relevant CWE: CWE-259 Use of Hard-coded Password

Metrics




CVSS Version
Base Score
Base Severity
Vector String




3.1
9.8
CRITICAL
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H








Acknowledgments

BIO-ISAC reported this vulnerability to CISA


Legal Notice and Terms of Use
This product is provided subject to this Notification (https://www.cisa.gov/notification) and this Privacy & Use policy (https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy).

Recommended Practices
CISA recommends users take defensive measures to minimize the risk of exploitation of this vulnerability.
Minimize network exposure for all control system devices and/or systems, ensuring they are not accessible from the internet.
Locate control system networks and remote devices behind firewalls and isolating them from business networks.
When remote access is required, use more secure methods, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), recognizing VPNs may have vulnerabilities and should be updated to the most current version available. Also recognize VPN is only as secure as the connected devices.
CISA reminds organizations to perform proper impact analysis and risk assessment prior to deploying defensive measures.
CISA also provides a section for control systems security recommended practices on the ICS webpage on cisa.gov/ics. Several CISA products detailing cyber defense best practices are available for reading and download, including Improving Industrial Control Systems Cybersecurity with Defense-in-Depth Strategies.
CISA encourages organizations to implement recommended cybersecurity strategies for proactive defense of ICS assets.
Additional mitigation guidance and recommended practices are publicly available on the ICS webpage at cisa.gov/ics in the technical information paper, ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber Intrusion Detection and Mitigation Strategies.
Organizations observing suspected malicious activity should follow established internal procedures and report findings to CISA for tracking and correlation against other incidents.
CISA also recommends users take the following measures to protect themselves from social engineering attacks:
Do not click web links or open attachments in unsolicited email messages.
Refer to Recognizing and Avoiding Email Scams for more information on avoiding email scams.
Refer to Avoiding Social Engineering and Phishing Attacks for more information on social engineering attacks.
No known public exploitation specifically targeting this vulnerability has been reported to CISA at this time.

vulnerability.title_ko: Eppendorf BioFlo 320
vulnerability.summary_ko:
CSAF 보기
요약
이 취약점을 성공적으로 악용하면 공격자가 생물반응기의 기능과 데이터에 대한 전체 액세스 권한을 얻을 수 있습니다.
영향을 받는 Eppendorf BioFlo 320 버전은 다음과 같습니다.

BioFlo 320 생물반응기 버전:all/*





CVSS
공급업체
장비
취약점




v3 9.8
에펜도르프
Eppendorf BioFlo 320
하드코딩된 비밀번호 사용




배경

중요 인프라 부문: 의료 및 공중 보건
배포 국가/지역: 전 세계
회사 본사 위치: 독일


취약점

모두 확장 +

CVE-2026-7251

해당 제품은 하드코딩된 비밀번호를 사용하는 VNC 서버로 인해 취약합니다. 원격 공격자가 원격 액세스가 활성화된 BioFlo 320 모델의 네트워크 주소를 알고 있는 경우, 이 비밀번호를 사용하여 사용자 인터페이스를 완전히 제어할 수 있습니다. 일단 연결되면 공격자는 BioFlo 320의 모든 제어판 기능에 대한 전체 액세스 권한을 갖게 됩니다. VNC 트래픽은 암호화되지 않습니다.
CVE 세부정보 보기

영향을 받는 제품
Eppendorf BioFlo 320

공급업체:Eppendorf
제품 버전:Eppendorf BioFlo 320 Bioreactor: vers:all/*
제품 상태:알려짐_영향을 받음


해결
MitigationEppendorf는 컨트롤러에서 VNC 액세스를 영구적으로 제거하는 소프트웨어 업데이트를 출시했습니다. 사용자는 https://www.eppendorf.com/software-downloads.https://www.eppendorf.com/software-downloads에서 이 업데이트를 다운로드하고 적용해야 합니다.
완화 영향을 받는 모든 BioFlo 320 시스템은 항상 기본적으로 VNC(Virtual Network Computing)가 비활성화된 상태로 배송되며 VNC는 타워에서 로컬로만 활성화될 수 있습니다. Eppendorf는 현재 모든 문서에서 VNC 구성 정보를 제거하여 BioFlo 320 사용 설명서에 더 이상 표시되지 않습니다.
완화Eppendorf는 사용자가 다음을 수행할 것을 권장합니다.컨트롤러에서 VNC가 비활성화되어 있는지 확인관리자 및 감독자 역할만 VNC 설정을 변경할 수 있도록 보안 활성화가능한 한 빨리 버전 5.0 소프트웨어 설치

관련 CWE: CWE-259 하드 코딩된 비밀번호 사용

측정항목




CVSS 버전
기본 점수
기본 심각도
벡터 문자열




3.1
9.8
심각
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H








감사의 말

BIO-ISAC는 이 취약점을 CISA에 보고했습니다.


법적 고지 및 이용 약관
이 제품은 본 공지(https://www.cisa.gov/notification) 및 개인정보 보호 및 사용 정책(https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy)에 따라 제공됩니다.

권장 사례
CISA는 사용자가 이 취약점의 악용 위험을 최소화하기 위해 방어 조치를 취할 것을 권장합니다.
모든 제어 시스템 장치 및/또는 시스템의 네트워크 노출을 최소화하여 인터넷에서 액세스할 수 없도록 합니다.
방화벽 뒤의 제어 시스템 네트워크와 원격 장치를 찾아 비즈니스 네트워크에서 격리합니다.
원격 액세스가 필요한 경우 VPN(가상 사설망)과 같은 보다 안전한 방법을 사용하여 VPN에 취약점이 있을 수 있음을 인식하고 사용 가능한 최신 버전으로 업데이트해야 합니다. 또한 VPN은 연결된 장치만큼만 안전하다는 것을 인식하세요.
CISA는 조직이 방어 조치를 취하기 전에 적절한 영향 분석 및 위험 평가를 수행할 것을 상기시킵니다.
CISA는 또한 cisa.gov/ics의 ICS 웹페이지에서 제어 시스템 보안 권장 사례에 대한 섹션을 제공합니다. 심층 방어 전략을 통한 산업 제어 시스템 사이버 보안 개선을 포함하여 사이버 방어 모범 사례를 자세히 설명하는 여러 CISA 제품을 읽고 다운로드할 수 있습니다.
CISA는 조직이 ICS 자산의 사전 예방적 방어를 위해 권장되는 사이버 보안 전략을 구현하도록 권장합니다.
추가 완화 지침 및 권장 사례는 ICS 웹페이지 cisa.gov/ics의 기술 정보 문서 ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber ​​Intrusion Protection and Mitigation Strategies에서 공개적으로 확인할 수 있습니다.
의심되는 악의적인 활동을 관찰하는 조직은 확립된 내부 절차를 따르고 다른 사건에 대한 추적 및 상관관계를 확인하기 위해 조사 결과를 CISA에 보고해야 합니다.
CISA는 또한 사용자에게 다음과 같은 조치를 취할 것을 권장합니다.
vulnerability.summary_3lines:
1. 제품 버전:Eppendorf BioFlo 320 Bioreactor: vers:all/* 
2. 심층 방어 전략을 통한 산업 제어 시스템 사이버 보안 개선을 포함하여 사이버 방어 모범 사
3. CISA는 또한 사용자가 사회 공학 공격으로부터 자신을 보호하기 위해 다음 조치를 취할 것

vulnerability.title: ABB Ability Camera Connect
vulnerability.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
ABB is aware of public reports of vulnerabilities in a 3rd party component VLC media player Version 2.2.4 which was delivered together with the installation package of Camera Connect Version 1.5.0.14 and below. An update is available that resolves a privately reported outdated 3rd party component with vulnerabilities in the product versions listed as affected in this advisory. An attacker who successfully exploited any of these vulnerabilities in the 3rd party component could potentially compromise the system in different ways.
The following versions of ABB Ability Camera Connect are affected:

Ability Camera Connect vers:intdot/<=1.5.0.14, 1.5.0.15





CVSS
Vendor
Equipment
Vulnerabilities




v3 9.8
ABB
ABB Ability Camera Connect
Heap-based Buffer Overflow, Integer Underflow (Wrap or Wraparound), Out-of-bounds Write, Uncontrolled Search Path Element, Integer Overflow or Wraparound, Off-by-one Error, Out-of-bounds Read, Double Free, Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer, Use After Free




Background

Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Chemical, Commercial Facilities, Communications, Critical Manufacturing, Energy, Transportation Systems
Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide
Company Headquarters Location: Switzerland


Vulnerabilities

Expand All +

CVE-2024-46461

VLC media player 3.0.20 and earlier is vulnerable to denial of service through an integer overflow which could be triggered with a maliciously crafted mms stream (heap based overflow). If successful, a malicious third party could trigger either a crash of VLC or an arbitrary code execution with the target user's privileges.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB Ability Camera Connect

Vendor:ABB
Product Version:ABB Ability Camera Connect <=1.5.0.14
Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
MitigationThe VLC-based component operates solely within completely isolated environments without internet access or any connectivity to external networks. Consequently: • No exposure to untrusted MMS streams: The integer overflow vulnerability relies on handling a maliciously crafted external stream, which is not possible in isolated environments • No remote attacker access: Without network ingress, attackers cannot trigger the vulnerability remotely. • Drastically reduced attack surface: The absence of any external media inputs effectively neutralizes the exploit path, significantly lowering the risk of both denial of service and code execution.
Vendor fixThe problem is corrected in the following product versions: ABB Ability Camera Connect 1.5.0.15 The 3rd party component has already been updated. The easiest path to mitigate the problem is an update of just VLC Media Player by the customer. ABB recommends that customers apply the update at earliest convenience. It is also possible to update to the latest Version of Camera Connect.

Relevant CWE: CWE-122 Heap-based Buffer Overflow

Vendor fixThe problem is corrected in the following product versions: ABB Ability Camera Connect 1.5.0.15 The 3rd party component has already been updated. The easiest path to mitigate the problem is an update of just VLC Media Player by the customer. ABB recommends that customers apply the update at earliest convenience. CVE-2023-47359

Videolan VLC prior to version 3.0.20 contains an incorrect offset read that leads to a Heap-Based Buffer Overflow in function GetPacket() and results in a memory corruption.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB Ability Camera Connect

Vendor:ABB
Product Version:ABB Ability Camera Connect <=1.5.0.14
Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
MitigationGiven that Camera Connect is deployed exclusively in fully isolated, air-gapped environments with no internet access or external network connectivity, the following risk-reduction factors apply: • No exposure to crafted MMS streams: The exploit requires the receipt of specially crafted packets via the MMS protocol, which cannot occur without network connectivity. • Network attack vector eliminated: As the vulnerability’s CVSS vector highlights a network-based attack (AV:N), the lack of any ingress network path nullifies the attack surface. • Low likelihood of exploitation: Without access to malicious media input, there is effectively no practical method for an attacker to trigger memory corruption, making the likelihood of denial of service or arbitrary code execution negligible.
Vendor fixThe problem is corrected in the following product versions: ABB Ability Camera Connect 1.5.0.15 The 3rd party component has already been updated. The easiest path to mitigate the problem is an update of just VLC Media Player by the customer. ABB recommends that customers apply the update at earliest convenience. It is also possible to update to the latest Version of Camera Connect.

CVE-2023-46814

A binary hijacking vulnerability exists within the VideoLAN VLC media player before 3.0.19 on Windows. The uninstaller attempts to execute code with elevated privileges out of a standard user writable location. Standard users may use this to gain arbitrary code execution as SYSTEM.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB Ability Camera Connect

Vendor:ABB
Product Version:ABB Ability Camera Connect <=1.5.0.14
Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
MitigationGiven that the VLC-based component is installed exclusively within air-gapped environments under strict administrative control, the following factors substantially reduce risk: • Restricted user access: Only trusted, privileged users perform installations and modifications. Standard users have no write permissions to the uninstaller directory. • No internet or network access: The exploit requires local manipulation of VLC’s uninstaller files; without external connectivity, remote coercion or manipulation is impossible. • Elimination of attacker vector: In air gapped deployments with administrative controls, un-privileged users cannot place malicious DLLs or executables in the uninstaller’s search path. • Minimized privilege escalation risk: The combination of controlled write access, absence of network exposure, and trusted user roles effectively neutralizes the binary hijacking threat, rendering successful exploitation highly unlikely.
Vendor fixThe problem is corrected in the following product versions: ABB Ability Camera Connect 1.5.0.15 The 3rd party component has already been updated. The easiest path to mitigate the problem is an update of just VLC Media Player by the customer. ABB recommends that customers apply the update at earliest convenience. It is also possible to update to the latest Version of Camera Connect.

Relevant CWE: CWE-427 Uncontrolled Search Path Element

Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
MitigationThe affected software is deployed exclusively in isolated environments with no internet connectivity and restricted external access. Exploitation of this vulnerability requires a user to open a specially crafted MKV file provided by an attacker. Since the system operates in a controlled network without exposure to untrusted sources, the likelihood of receiving and executing malicious media files is significantly reduced. Additionally, operational procedures can enforce the use of trusted media files only, further minimizing the risk.
Vendor fixThe problem is corrected in the following product versions: ABB Ability Camera Connect 1.5.0.15 The 3rd party component has already been updated. The easiest path to mitigate the problem is an update of just VLC Media Player by the customer. ABB recommends that customers apply the update at earliest convenience. It is also possible to update to the latest Version of Camera Connect.

Relevant CWE: CWE-416 Use After Free

This document and parts hereof must not be reproduced or copied without written permission from ABB, and the contents hereof must not be imparted to a third party nor used for any unauthorized purpose. All rights to registrations and trademarks reside with their respective owners.

Frequently Asked Questions
What causes the vulnerability? The vulnerability is caused by unchecked input data in the VLC media player in Camera Connect What is ? VLC media player What might an attacker use the vulnerability to do? An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could cause the affected system node to stop or become inaccessible and allow the attacker to insert and run arbitrary code . How could an attacker exploit the vulnerability? An attacker could try to exploit the vulnerability by creating a specially crafted file, copying the file to affected system nodes and then manually open the file via VLC Mediaplayer. This would require that the attacker has access to the system network, by connecting to the network either directly or through a wrongly configured or penetrated firewall, or that he installs malicious software on a system node or otherwise infects the network with malicious software. Recommended practices help mitigate such attacks, see section Mitigating Factors above. Could the vulnerability be exploited remotely? No, to exploit this vulnerability an attacker would need to have physical access to an affected system node. Can functional safety be affected by an exploit of this vulnerability? While these vulnerabilities primarily impact confidentiality, integrity, and availability, they do not directly affect functional safety in the traditional sense. However, a compromised system (due to arbitrary code execution or a critical service crash) may compromise safety-related processes dependent on VLC, potentially disrupting business operations or compliance. What does the update do? The update removes the vulnerability by providing a newer version of the VLC media player When this security advisory was issued, had this vulnerability been publicly disclosed? This vulnerability has been publicly disclosed for the 3rd party component, but not for the ABB product using this component When this security advisory was issued, had ABB received any reports that this vulnerability was being exploited? No, ABB had not received any information indicating that this vulnerability had been exploited when this security advisory was originally issued.

Legal Notice and Terms of Use
This product is provided subject to this Notification (https://www.cisa.gov/notification) and this Privacy & Use policy (https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy).

Recommended Practices
CISA recommends users take defensive measures to minimize the exploitation risk of this vulnerability.
Minimize network exposure for all control system devices and/or systems, and ensure they are not accessible from the internet.
Locate control system networks and remote devices behind firewalls and isolate them from business networks.
When remote access is required, use more secure methods, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), recognizing VPNs may have vulnerabilities and should be updated to the most recent version available. Also recognize VPN is only as secure as its connected devices.
CISA reminds organizations to perform proper impact analysis and risk assessment prior to deploying defensive measures.
CISA also provides a section for control systems security recommended practices on the ICS webpage on cisa.gov. Several CISA products detailing cyber defense best practices are available for reading and download, including Improving Industrial Control Systems Cybersecurity with Defense-in-Depth Strategies.
CISA encourages organizations to implement recommended cybersecurity strategies for proactive defense of ICS assets. Additional mitigation guidance and recommended practices are publicly available on the ICS webpage at cisa.gov in the technical information paper, ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber Intrusion Detection and Mitigation Strategies.
Organizations observing suspected malicious activity should follow established internal procedures and report findings to CISA for tracking and correlation against other incidents.

Advisory Conversion Disclaimer
This ICSA is a verbatim republication of ABB PSIRT 4HZM000603 from a direct conversion of the vendor's Common Security Advisory Framework (CSAF) advisory. This is republished to CISA's website as a means of increasing visibility and is provided "as-is" for informational purposes only. vulnerability.title_ko: ABB 능력 카메라 연결
vulnerability.summary_ko:
CSAF 보기
요약
ABB는 Camera Connect 버전 1.5.0.14 이하의 설치 패키지와 함께 제공되는 타사 구성 요소 VLC 미디어 플레이어 버전 2.2.4의 취약점에 대한 공개 보고서를 알고 있습니다. 이 권고에 영향을 받는 것으로 나열된 제품 버전의 취약점이 있는 비공개적으로 보고된 오래된 타사 구성 요소를 해결하는 업데이트가 제공됩니다. 타사 구성 요소의 이러한 취약점을 성공적으로 악용한 공격자는 잠재적으로 다양한 방식으로 시스템을 손상시킬 수 있습니다.
영향을 받는 ABB Ability Camera Connect 버전은 다음과 같습니다.

능력 카메라 연결 버전:intdot/<=1.5.0.14, 1.5.0.15





CVSS
공급업체
장비
취약점




v3 9.8
ABB
ABB 능력 카메라 연결
힙 기반 버퍼 오버플로, 정수 언더플로(랩 또는 랩어라운드), 범위를 벗어난 쓰기, 제어되지 않은 검색 경로 요소, 정수 오버플로 또는 랩어라운드, 오프 바이 원 오류, 범위를 벗어난 읽기, 이중 해제, 메모리 버퍼 범위 내에서 작업에 대한 부적절한 제한, 해제 후 사용




배경

중요 인프라 부문: 화학, 상업 시설, 통신, 중요 제조, 에너지, 운송 시스템
배포 국가/지역: 전 세계
회사 본사 위치: 스위스


취약점

모두 확장 +

CVE-2024-46461

VLC 미디어 플레이어 3.0.20 이하 버전은 악의적으로 제작된 mms 스트림(힙 기반 오버플로)으로 인해 발생할 수 있는 정수 오버플로를 통한 서비스 거부에 취약합니다. 성공할 경우 악의적인 제3자가 VLC 충돌을 일으키거나 대상 사용자의 권한으로 임의 코드를 실행할 수 있습니다.
CVE 세부정보 보기

영향을 받는 제품
ABB 능력 카메라 연결

공급업체:ABB
제품 버전:ABB Ability Camera Connect <=1.5.0.14
제품 상태:수정됨, 알려진_영향을 받음


해결
완화 VLC 기반 구성 요소는 인터넷 액세스나 외부 네트워크에 대한 연결 없이 완전히 격리된 환경 내에서만 작동합니다. 결과: • 신뢰할 수 없는 MMS 스트림에 노출되지 않음: 정수 오버플로 취약점은 악의적으로 제작된 외부 스트림 처리에 의존하며 이는 격리된 환경에서는 불가능합니다. • 원격 공격자 액세스 없음: 네트워크 수신이 없으면 공격자는 취약점을 원격으로 실행할 수 없습니다. • 공격 표면 대폭 감소: 외부 미디어 입력이 없기 때문에 악용 경로가 효과적으로 무력화되어 서비스 거부 및 코드 실행 위험이 크게 낮아집니다.
공급업체 수정 문제는 다음 제품 버전에서 수정되었습니다. ABB Ability Camera Connect 1.5.0.15 타사 구성 요소가 이미 업데이트되었습니다. 문제를 완화하는 가장 쉬운 방법은 고객이 VLC Media Player만 업데이트하는 것입니다. ABB는 고객이 최대한 빠른 시일 내에 업데이트를 적용할 것을 권장합니다. 최신 버전의 Camera Connect로 업데이트하는 것도 가능합니다.

관련 CWE: CWE-122 힙 기반 버퍼 오버플로

버전 3.0.20 이전의 Videolan VLC에는 잘못된 패킷 길이로 이어지는 정수 언더플로우가 포함되어 있습니다.

해결
완화• Air-Gapped 환경만 해당: Camera Connect는 네트워크 연결이나 인터넷 액세스가 전혀 없는 완전히 격리된 환경에 배포됩니다. • MMS 스트림에 노출되지 않음: 취약점은 시스템이 에어갭 상태일 때 외부 또는 내부 네트워크 소스에서 발생할 수 없는 가공된 MMS 스트림 처리에 따라 달라집니다. • 원격 공격 표면 제거: 공격자가 악의적인 미디어 입력을 전달할 수 있는 방법이 없으면 취약점을 원격으로 실행할 수 없습니다. • 악용 위험의 대폭 감소: 외부 미디어 수집이 없고 사용이 불가능함

vulnerability.title: ABB B&R Automation Runtime DoS Vulnerability in System Diagnostics Manager (SDM)
vulnerability.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
An update is available that resolves a vulnerability identified by B&Rs internal security analysis in the product versions listed as affected in this advisory. An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could cause the product to stop.
The following versions of ABB B&R Automation Runtime DoS Vulnerability in System Diagnostics Manager (SDM) are affected:

Automation Runtime <6.3
Automation Runtime <Q4.93





CVSS
Vendor
Equipment
Vulnerabilities




v3 10
B&R
ABB B&R Automation Runtime DoS Vulnerability in System Diagnostics Manager (SDM)
Improper Resource Locking




Background

Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Chemical, Communications, Critical Manufacturing, Dams, Energy, Healthcare and Public Health, Information Technology, Water and Wastewater
Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide
Company Headquarters Location: Switzerland


Vulnerabilities

Expand All +

CVE-2025-3450

An Improper Resource Locking vulnerability in the SDM component of B&R Automation Runtime versions before 6.3 and before Q4.93 may allow an unauthenticated network-based attacker to delete data causing denial of service conditions.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB B&R Automation Runtime DoS Vulnerability in System Diagnostics Manager (SDM)

Vendor:B&R
Product Version:Automation Runtime <6.3, Automation Runtime <Q4.93
Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
Vendor fixThe problem is corrected in Automation Runtime versions 6.3 and Q4.93. The System Diagnostic Manager (SDM) is disabled by default in Automation Runtime 6 and is not in-tended be enabled on active systems located outside properly secured production networks or in facilities lacking adequate physical and logical access controls to prevent any form of unauthorized interaction. For customers who use SDM on their systems, B&R recommends applying the update at the earliest convenience. The process to install updates is described in the user manual. The step to identify the installed product version is described in the user manual.

Relevant CWE: CWE-413 Improper Resource Locking

Metrics




CVSS Version
Base Score
Base Severity
Vector String

CRITICAL

ABB PSIRT reported this vulnerability to CISA.

The information in this document is subject to change without notice, and should not be construed as a commitment by B&R. B&R provides no warranty, express or implied, including warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, for the information contained in this document, and assumes no responsibility for any errors that may appear in this document. This document and parts hereof must not be reproduced or copied without written permission from B&R, and the contents hereof must not be imparted to a third party nor used for any unauthorized purpose. All rights to registrations and trademarks reside with their respective owners.

Mitigating factors
Deactivate the vulnerable component The SDM is deactivated by default on Automation Runtime version >=6.0. For Automation Runtime versions <6.0, the SDM can be deactivated in the Automation Studio project. Please refer to Automation Help GUID 1d915d67-07f7-4034-a472-c204b5cabbfe for further guidance. Access to the System Diagnostic Manager (SDM) shall be restricted to trusted personnel through appropriate external security measures. If SDM is required solely for maintenance purposes, it should be enabled or access granted only for the minimum time necessary to perform the task. Limit accessibility B&R recommends in general to configure the HTTP protocol over TLS (HTTPS). Customers may restrict access to the webserver by configuring mutual TLS (mTLS) in the Automation Studio project (Option “Validate SSL communication partner”). Be aware that configuring mTLS would impact also other applications using the AR webserver (e.g. mapp View). Please refer to Automation Help GUID 01ced6c0-28ef-4aaa-bd05-2442b971859c to learn more about the TLS Configuration in Automation Studio. In addition, accessibility of the webserver can be limited to trusted IP addresses using the Automation Runtime host-based firewall. Please refer to Automation Help GUID 75b8994b-f97a-4e0f-8278-43c7a737e65f for details. Refer to section “General security recommendations” for further advise on how to keep your system secure.

Frequently asked questions
What causes the vulnerability? The vulnerabilities are caused by improper resource locking. What is System Diagnostics Manager (SDM)? System Diagnostics Manager (SDM) is a webpage available over the Automation Runtime Webserver, showing key diagnostic information of the running controller What is Automation Runtime (AR)? B&R Automation Runtime is a middleware system enabling customers to run applications on B&R target systems. What might an attacker use the vulnerability to do? An attacker who successfully exploited these vulnerabilities could cause the affected system node to stop. How could an attacker exploit the vulnerability? An attacker could try to exploit the vulnerability by creating a specially crafted message and sending the message to an affected system node. This would require that the attacker has access to the system network, by connecting to the network either directly or through a wrongly configured or penetrated firewall, or that he installs malicious software on a system node or otherwise infects the network with malicious software. Recommended practices help mitigate such attacks, see section Mitigating Factors above. Could the vulnerability be exploited remotely? Yes, an attacker who has network access to an affected system node could exploit this vulnerability. Recommended practices include that process control systems are physically protected, have no direct connections to the Internet, and are separated from other networks by means of a firewall system that has a minimal number of ports exposed. When this security advisory was issued, had this vulnerability been publicly disclosed? No, B&R discovered the vulnerabilities through its own security analysis. When this security advisory was issued, had B&R received any reports that this vulnerability was being exploited? No, B&R had not received any information indicating that this vulnerability had been exploited when this security advisory was originally issued.

Recommended Practices
CISA recommends users take defensive measures to minimize the exploitation risk of these vulnerabilities.
Minimize network exposure for all control system devices and/or systems, and ensure they are not accessible from the internet.
Locate control system networks and remote devices behind firewalls and isolate them from business networks.
When remote access is required, use more secure methods, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), recognizing VPNs may have vulnerabilities and should be updated to the most recent version available. Also recognize VPN is only as secure as its connected devices.
CISA reminds organizations to perform proper impact analysis and risk assessment prior to deploying defensive measures.
CISA also provides a section for control systems security recommended practices on the ICS webpage on cisa.gov. Several CISA products detailing cyber defense best practices are available for reading and download, including Improving Industrial Control Systems Cybersecurity with Defense-in-Depth Strategies.
CISA encourages organizations to implement recommended cybersecurity strategies for proactive defense of ICS assets. Additional mitigation guidance and recommended practices are publicly available on the ICS webpage at cisa.gov in the technical information paper, ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber Intrusion Detection and Mitigation Strategies.
Organizations observing suspected malicious activity should follow established internal procedures and report findings to CISA for tracking and correlation against other incidents.

vulnerability.title_ko: 시스템 진단 관리자(SDM)의 ABB B&R 자동화 런타임 DoS 취약점
vulnerability.summary_ko:
CSAF 보기
요약
이 권고에 영향을 받는 것으로 나열된 제품 버전에서 B&R의 내부 보안 분석에 의해 식별된 취약점을 해결하는 업데이트가 제공됩니다. 이 취약점 악용에 성공한 공격자는 제품을 중지시킬 수 있습니다.
시스템 진단 관리자(SDM)의 ABB B&R 자동화 런타임 DoS 취약점의 다음 버전이 영향을 받습니다.

자동화 런타임 <6.3
자동화 런타임 <Q4.93





CVSS
공급업체
장비
취약점




v3 10
비앤알
시스템 진단 관리자(SDM)의 ABB B&R 자동화 런타임 DoS 취약점
부적절한 리소스 잠금




배경

중요 인프라 부문: 화학, 통신, 중요 제조, 댐, 에너지, 의료 및 공중 보건, 정보 기술, 수자원 및 폐수
배포 국가/지역: 전 세계
회사 본사 위치: 스위스


취약점

모두 확장 +

CVE-2025-3450

6.3 이전 및 Q4.93 이전 버전의 B&R Automation Runtime SDM 구성 요소에 있는 부적절한 리소스 잠금 취약점으로 인해 인증되지 않은 네트워크 기반 공격자가 데이터를 삭제할 수 있어 서비스 거부 조건이 발생할 수 있습니다.
CVE 세부정보 보기

영향을 받는 제품
시스템 진단 관리자(SDM)의 ABB B&R 자동화 런타임 DoS 취약점

공급업체:B&R
제품 버전:자동화 런타임 <6.3, 자동화 런타임 <Q4.93
제품 상태:수정됨, 알려진_영향을 받음


해결
공급업체 수정 문제는 Automation Runtime 버전 6.3 및 Q4.93에서 수정되었습니다. System Diagnostic Manager(SDM)는 Automation Runtime 6에서 기본적으로 비활성화되어 있으며 적절하게 보안된 생산 네트워크 외부에 있는 활성 시스템이나 모든 형태의 무단 상호 작용을 방지하기 위한 적절한 물리적 및 논리적 액세스 제어가 부족한 시설에서는 활성화되지 않습니다. 시스템에서 SDM을 사용하는 고객의 경우 B&R은 최대한 빠른 시일 내에 업데이트를 적용할 것을 권장합니다. 업데이트 설치 과정은 사용 설명서에 설명되어 있습니다. 설치된 제품 버전을 확인하는 단계는 사용 설명서에 설명되어 있습니다.

관련 CWE: CWE-413 부적절한 리소스 잠금

측정항목




CVSS 버전
기본 점수
기본 심각도
벡터 문자열




3.1
10
심각
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:N/I:H/A:H/E:U/RL:O/RC:C








감사의 말

ABB PSIRT는 이 취약점을 CISA에 보고했습니다.


공지사항
이 문서의 정보는 예고 없이 변경될 수 있으며 B&R의 약속으로 해석되어서는 안 됩니다. B&R은 이 문서에 포함된 정보에 대해 상품성 및 특정 목적에의 적합성에 대한 보증을 포함하여 명시적이거나 묵시적인 어떠한 보증도 제공하지 않으며 이 문서에 나타날 수 있는 모든 오류에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. 어떠한 경우에도 B&R 또는 그 공급업체는 이 문서의 사용이나 이 문서에 설명된 하드웨어 또는 소프트웨어의 사용으로 인해 발생하는 모든 성격이나 종류의 직접적, 간접적, 특수적, 우발적 또는 결과적 손해에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. 이는 B&R 또는 그 공급업체가 그러한 손해의 가능성을 통보받은 경우에도 마찬가지입니다. 이 문서와 그 일부는 B&R의 서면 승인 없이 복제 또는 복사할 수 없으며, 이 문서의 내용은 제3자에게 전달되거나 승인되지 않은 목적으로 사용되어서는 안 됩니다. 등록 및 상표에 대한 모든 권리는 해당 소유자에게 있습니다.

완화 요인
취약한 구성 요소 비활성화 SDM은 Automation Runtime 버전 >=6.0에서 기본적으로 비활성화됩니다. Automation Runtime 버전 <6.0의 경우 Automation Studio 프로젝트에서 SDM을 비활성화할 수 있습니다. 자세한 지침은 자동화 도움말 GUID 1d915d67-07f7-4034-a472-c204b5cabbfe를 참조하세요. 시스템 진단 관리자(SDM)에 대한 액세스는 적절한 외부 보안 조치를 통해 신뢰할 수 있는 직원으로 제한되어야 합니다. SDM이 유지 관리 목적으로만 필요한 경우 작업을 수행하는 데 필요한 최소 시간 동안만 SDM을 활성화하거나 액세스 권한을 부여해야 합니다. 접근성 제한 B&R은 일반적으로 TL을 통해 HTTP 프로토콜을 구성할 것을 권장합니다.

vulnerability.title: ABB LVS MConfig
vulnerability.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
ABB became aware of an internally discovered vulnerability in the MConfig product versions listed as affected in the advisory. An attacker with access to local networks who successfully exploits vulnerability could have access to application’s sensitive information. ABB strongly advises customers to update MConfig with latest software version.
The following versions of ABB LVS MConfig are affected:

LVS <=1.4.9.21





CVSS
Vendor
Equipment
Vulnerabilities




v3 7.4
ABB
ABB LVS MConfig
Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Memory




Background

Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Chemical, Critical Manufacturing, Energy, Food and Agriculture, Transportation Systems, Water and Wastewater
Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide
Company Headquarters Location: Switzerland


Vulnerabilities

Expand All +

CVE-2025-9970

During the runtime of the MConfig Software application, an attacker can export the memory dump file into the operating system. If passwords are stored in plain text in memory, they will be included in these dump files. If such dump files are mishandled, attackers could obtain them and extract the passwords.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB LVS MConfig

Vendor:ABB
Product Version:MConfig Version <=1.4.9.21
Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
Vendor fixThe vulnerability is resolved in the following product versions: MConfig version 1.4.9.22 ABB advises users to update their devices to the latest software version. Additionally, ABB recommends implementing defensive measures to reduce the risk of vulnerability exploitation, as outlined in the product instruction manual. Please refer to the section “Mitigation factors” for more information

Relevant CWE: CWE-316 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Memory

Metrics




CVSS Version
Base Score
Base Severity
Vector String




3.1
7.4
HIGH
CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:H/A:H/E:P/RL:O/RC:C/CR:L/IR:L/AR:L








Acknowledgments

ABB PSIRT reported this vulnerability to CISA.


Notice
The information in this document is subject to change without notice, and should not be construed as a commitment by ABB. ABB provides no warranty, express or implied, including warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, for the information contained in this document, and assumes no responsibility for any errors that may appear in this document. In no event shall ABB or any of its suppliers be liable for direct, indirect, special, incidental or consequential damages of any nature or kind arising from the use of this document, or from the use of any hardware or software described in this document, even if ABB or its suppliers have been advised of the possibility of such damages. This document and parts hereof must not be reproduced or copied without written permission from ABB, and the contents hereof must not be imparted to a third-party nor used for any unauthorized purpose. All rights to registrations and trademarks reside with their respective owners.

Mitigating factors
Mitigating factors describe conditions and circumstances that make an attack that exploits the vulnerability difficult or less likely to succeed. In case customer cannot upgrade the firmware or it is not feasible then please immediately apply mitigating factors mentioned in “General security recommendations”.

Frequently asked questions
What causes the vulnerability? The vulnerability is caused by code defect allowing the attacker to extract the sensitive information such as user credentials from memory dump of the application. Please refer to Vulnerability severity and details for further details. What is MConfig ? MConfig is the parameterizing software for ABB LV switchgear components such as motor and feeder controller, operation panel, temperature monitoring solutions and protocol converter. The components are physically installed in a low voltage switchgear located in switch rooms that require authority to access. To run this software on a host machine (computer), the operating system should be Win11 or later version. What might an attacker use the vulnerability to do? If the mentioned vulnerability has been successfully exploited by an attacker, this could allow the attacker to extract sensitive information such as user credentials. With user credentials and access to a host machine with MConfig installed, and access to the switch room with components installed in a switchgear, the attacker can modify the setting of the components potentially compromising its correct operation. How could an attacker exploit vulnerability? An attacker with host machine physical access could, after a user log into MConfig, exploit a vulnerability by exporting a memory dump during runtime, potentially exposing the user's password. Could vulnerability be exploited remotely? The vulnerability can only be exploited if an attacker has physical access to the host machine with MConfig software. What does the update do? MConfig version V1.4.9.22 update has fix for the vulnerability mentioned in Vulnerability severity and details section. The measures below were implemented to fix the vulnerability: • Clear any authentication-related memory data after a successful login. • Hash the passwords in SHA256

Legal Notice and Terms of Use
This product is provided subject to this Notification (https://www.cisa.gov/notification) and this Privacy & Use policy (https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy).

Recommended Practices
CISA recommends users take defensive measures to minimize the exploitation risk of these vulnerabilities.
Minimize network exposure for all control system devices and/or systems, and ensure they are not accessible from the internet.
Locate control system networks and remote devices behind firewalls and isolate them from business networks.
When remote access is required, use more secure methods, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), recognizing VPNs may have vulnerabilities and should be updated to the most recent version available. Also recognize VPN is only as secure as its connected devices.
CISA reminds organizations to perform proper impact analysis and risk assessment prior to deploying defensive measures.
CISA also provides a section for control systems security recommended practices on the ICS webpage on cisa.gov. Several CISA products detailing cyber defense best practices are available for reading and download, including Improving Industrial Control Systems Cybersecurity with Defense-in-Depth Strategies.
CISA encourages organizations to implement recommended cybersecurity strategies for proactive defense of ICS assets. Additional mitigation guidance and recommended practices are publicly available on the ICS webpage at cisa.gov in the technical information paper, ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber Intrusion Detection and Mitigation Strategies.
Organizations observing suspected malicious activity should follow established internal procedures and report findings to CISA for tracking and correlation against other incidents.

This ICSA is a verbatim republication of ABB PSIRT 4TZ00000006008 from a direct conversion of the vendor's Common Security Advisory Framework (CSAF) advisory. This is republished to CISA's website as a means of increasing visibility and is provided "as-is" for informational purposes only. 요약
ABB는 권고에 영향을 받는 것으로 나열된 MConfig 제품 버전에서 내부적으로 발견된 취약점을 인지하게 되었습니다. 취약점 악용에 성공한 로컬 네트워크에 대한 액세스 권한이 있는 공격자는 애플리케이션의 민감한 정보에 액세스할 수 있습니다. ABB는 고객에게 최신 소프트웨어 버전으로 MConfig를 업데이트할 것을 강력히 권고합니다.

ABB

배경

중요 인프라 부문: 화학, 중요 제조, 에너지, 식품 및 농업, 운송 시스템, 수자원 및 폐수

공지사항
본 문서의 정보는 예고 없이 변경될 수 있으며 ABB의 약속으로 해석되어서는 안 됩니다. ABB는 상품성 및 특정 목적에의 적합성에 대한 보증을 포함하여 본 문서에 포함된 정보에 대해 명시적이거나 묵시적인 어떠한 보증도 제공하지 않으며, 본 문서에 나타날 수 있는 오류에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. 어떠한 경우에도 ABB 또는 그 공급업체는 본 문서의 사용이나 본 문서에 설명된 하드웨어나 소프트웨어의 사용으로 인해 발생하는 모든 성격이나 종류의 직접적, 간접적, 특수적, 우발적 또는 파생적 손해에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. ABB 또는 그 공급업체가 그러한 손해의 가능성을 통보받은 경우에도 마찬가지입니다. 이 문서와 그 일부는 ABB의 서면 승인 없이 복제 또는 복사할 수 없으며, 이 문서의 내용을 제3자에게 전달하거나 승인되지 않은 목적으로 사용해서는 안 됩니다. 등록 및 상표에 대한 모든 권리는 해당 소유자에게 있습니다.

완화 요인
완화 요소는 취약점을 악용하는 공격을 어렵게 하거나 성공 가능성을 낮추는 조건 및 상황을 설명합니다. 고객이 펌웨어를 업그레이드할 수 없거나 실행 가능하지 않은 경우 "일반 보안 권장 사항"에 언급된 완화 요소를 즉시 적용하십시오.

자주 묻는 질문
취약점의 원인은 무엇입니까? 이 취약점은 공격자가 애플리케이션의 메모리 덤프에서 사용자 자격 증명과 같은 민감한 정보를 추출할 수 있도록 허용하는 코드 결함으로 인해 발생합니다. 자세한 내용은 취약점 심각도 및 세부정보를 참조하세요. MConfig란 무엇입니까? MConfig는 모터 및 피더 컨트롤러, 작동 패널, 온도 모니터링 솔루션 및 프로토콜 변환기와 같은 ABB LV 스위치기어 구성 요소를 위한 매개변수화 소프트웨어입니다. 구성요소는 액세스 권한이 필요한 스위치룸에 위치한 저전압 스위치기어에 물리적으로 설치됩니다. 이 소프트웨어를 실행하려면 o

1. 본 문서의 정보는 예고 없이 변경될 수 있으며 ABB의 약속으로 해석되어서는 안 됩니다.
2. 모든 제어 시스템 장치 및/또는 시스템의 네트워크 노출을 최소화하고 인터넷에서 액세스할 수
3. 심층 방어 전략을 통한 산업 제어 시스템 사이버 보안 개선을 포함하여 사이버 방어 모범 사

vulnerability.title: ABB AC500 V2
vulnerability.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
ABB became aware of vulnerabilities in AC500 V2 listed as affected in the advisory. An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could access fragments of Modbus telegrams that have been sent earlier by that PLC
The following versions of ABB AC500 V2 are affected:

AC500 V2 <=2.5.2, 2.5.3





CVSS
Vendor
Equipment
Vulnerabilities




v3 5.8
ABB
ABB AC500 V2
Buffer Over-read




Background

Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Critical Manufacturing, Energy, Water and Wastewater
Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide
Company Headquarters Location: Switzerland


Vulnerabilities

Expand All +

CVE-2025-7745

Sending unsupported function codes to the AC500 V2 Modbus server might result in invalid responses. Fragments of previous responses might be added to the end of the response.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB AC500 V2

Vendor:ABB
Product Version:ABB AC500 V2 <=2.5.2
Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
Vendor fixThe vulnerabilities have been resolved in the following product versions: AC500 V2 firmware version 2.5.3 (released in 2016) and later

Relevant CWE: CWE-126 Buffer Over-read

Metrics




CVSS Version
Base Score
Base Severity
Vector String




3.1
5.8
MEDIUM
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:N/A:N








Acknowledgments

Reid Wightman of Dragos. Inc reported these vulnerabilities to Schneider Electric.


Notice
The information in this document is subject to change without notice, and should not be construed as a commitment by ABB. ABB provides no warranty, express or implied, including warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, for the information contained in this document, and assumes no responsibility for any errors that may appear in this document. In no event shall ABB or any of its suppliers be liable for direct, indirect, special, incidental or consequential damages of any nature or kind arising from the use of this document, or from the use of any hardware or software described in this document, even if ABB or its suppliers have been advised of the possibility of such damages. This document and parts hereof must not be reproduced or copied without written permission from ABB, and the contents hereof must not be imparted to a third party nor used for any unauthorized purpose. All rights to registrations and trademarks reside with their respective owners.

Mitigating factors
Mitigating factors describe conditions and circumstances that make an attack that exploits the vulnerability difficult or less likely to succeed. Regarding this vulnerability it is recommended to • Do not use the Modbus server for sending any sensitive data, as fragments might be accessible even after the initial sending of the response • Only use supported Modbus function codes, as invalid responses to unsupported function codes might have negative effects on the requesting Modbus client. Refer to section “General security recommendations” for further advise on how to keep your system secure.

Legal Notice and Terms of Use
This product is provided subject to this Notification (https://www.cisa.gov/notification) and this Privacy & Use policy (https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy).

Recommended Practices
CISA recommends users take defensive measures to minimize the exploitation risk of these vulnerabilities.
Minimize network exposure for all control system devices and/or systems, and ensure they are not accessible from the internet.
Locate control system networks and remote devices behind firewalls and isolate them from business networks.
When remote access is required, use more secure methods, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), recognizing VPNs may have vulnerabilities and should be updated to the most recent version available. Also recognize VPN is only as secure as its connected devices.
CISA reminds organizations to perform proper impact analysis and risk assessment prior to deploying defensive measures.
CISA also provides a section for control systems security recommended practices on the ICS webpage on cisa.gov. Several CISA products detailing cyber defense best practices are available for reading and download, including Improving Industrial Control Systems Cybersecurity with Defense-in-Depth Strategies.
CISA encourages organizations to implement recommended cybersecurity strategies for proactive defense of ICS assets. Additional mitigation guidance and recommended practices are publicly available on the ICS webpage at cisa.gov in the technical information paper, ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber Intrusion Detection and Mitigation Strategies.
Organizations observing suspected malicious activity should follow established internal procedures and report findings to CISA for tracking and correlation against other incidents.

Advisory Conversion Disclaimer
This ICSA is a verbatim republication of ABB PSIRT 3ADR011432 from a direct conversion of the vendor's Common Security Advisory Framework (CSAF) advisory. This is republished to CISA's website as a means of increasing visibility and is provided "as-is" for informational purposes only. vulnerability.title_ko: ABB AC500 V2
vulnerability.summary_ko:
CSAF 보기
요약
ABB는 권고에 영향을 받는 것으로 나열된 AC500 V2의 취약점을 인지하게 되었습니다. 이 취약점 악용에 성공한 공격자는 해당 PLC가 이전에 전송한 Modbus 텔레그램 조각에 액세스할 수 있습니다.
ABB AC500 V2의 다음 버전이 영향을 받습니다.

AC500 V2 <=2.5.2, 2.5.3





CVSS
공급업체
장비
취약점




v3 5.8
ABB
ABB AC500 V2
버퍼 초과 읽기




배경

중요 인프라 부문: 중요 제조, 에너지, 수자원 및 폐수
배포 국가/지역: 전 세계
회사 본사 위치: 스위스


취약점

모두 확장 +

CVE-2025-7745

지원되지 않는 기능 코드를 AC500 V2 Modbus 서버로 보내면 잘못된 응답이 발생할 수 있습니다. 이전 응답의 일부가 응답 끝에 추가될 수 있습니다.
CVE 세부정보 보기

영향을 받는 제품
ABB AC500 V2

공급업체:ABB
제품 버전:ABB AC500 V2 <=2.5.2
제품 상태:수정됨, 알려진_영향을 받음


해결
공급업체 수정취약성은 다음 제품 버전에서 해결되었습니다. AC500 V2 펌웨어 버전 2.5.3(2016년 출시) 이상

관련 CWE: CWE-126 버퍼 오버리드

측정항목




CVSS 버전
기본 점수
기본 심각도
벡터 문자열




3.1
5.8
중간
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:N/A:N








감사의 말

드라고스의 리드 와이트먼. Inc는 Schneider Electric에 이러한 취약점을 보고했습니다.


공지사항
본 문서의 정보는 예고 없이 변경될 수 있으며 ABB의 약속으로 해석되어서는 안 됩니다. ABB는 상품성 및 특정 목적에의 적합성에 대한 보증을 포함하여 본 문서에 포함된 정보에 대해 명시적이거나 묵시적인 어떠한 보증도 제공하지 않으며, 본 문서에 나타날 수 있는 오류에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. 어떠한 경우에도 ABB 또는 그 공급업체는 본 문서의 사용이나 본 문서에 설명된 하드웨어나 소프트웨어의 사용으로 인해 발생하는 모든 성격이나 종류의 직접적, 간접적, 특수적, 우발적 또는 파생적 손해에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. ABB 또는 그 공급업체가 그러한 손해의 가능성을 통보받은 경우에도 마찬가지입니다. 이 문서와 그 일부는 ABB의 서면 허가 없이 복제하거나 복사할 수 없으며, 이 문서의 내용을 제3자에게 전달하거나 승인되지 않은 목적으로 사용해서는 안 됩니다. 등록 및 상표에 대한 모든 권리는 해당 소유자에게 있습니다.

완화 요인
완화 요소는 취약점을 악용하는 공격을 어렵게 하거나 성공 가능성을 낮추는 조건 및 상황을 설명합니다. 이 취약점과 관련하여 다음을 권장합니다. • 응답을 처음 보낸 후에도 조각에 액세스할 수 있으므로 민감한 데이터를 보내는 데 Modbus 서버를 사용하지 마십시오. • 지원되지 않는 기능 코드에 대한 유효하지 않은 응답은 요청하는 Modbus 클라이언트에 부정적인 영향을 미칠 수 있으므로 지원되는 Modbus 기능 코드만 사용하십시오. 시스템 보안을 유지하는 방법에 대한 자세한 내용은 "일반 보안 권장 사항" 섹션을 참조하세요.

법적 고지 및 이용 약관
이 제품은 본 공지(https://www.cisa.gov/notification) 및 개인정보 보호 및 사용 정책(https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy)에 따라 제공됩니다.

권장 사례
CISA는 사용자가 이러한 취약점의 악용 위험을 최소화하기 위해 방어 조치를 취할 것을 권장합니다.
모든 제어 시스템 장치 및/또는 시스템의 네트워크 노출을 최소화하고 인터넷에서 액세스할 수 없도록 하십시오.
방화벽 뒤의 제어 시스템 네트워크와 원격 장치를 찾아 비즈니스 네트워크에서 격리합니다.
원격 액세스가 필요한 경우 VPN(가상 사설망)과 같은 보다 안전한 방법을 사용하여 VPN에 취약점이 있을 수 있음을 인식하고 사용 가능한 최신 버전으로 업데이트해야 합니다. 또한 VPN은 연결된 장치만큼만 안전하다는 것을 인식하세요.
CISA는 조직이 방어 조치를 취하기 전에 적절한 영향 분석 및 위험 평가를 수행할 것을 상기시킵니다.
CISA는 또한 보안 제어 시스템에 대한 섹션도 제공합니다.

vulnerability.title: ABB Terra AC
vulnerability.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
ABB is aware of vulnerabilities in the product versions listed as affected in the advisory. An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could cause the pollution of heap memory which potentially takes remote control of the product and performs a write operation to the flash memory to alter the firmware behavior.
The following versions of ABB Terra AC are affected:

Terra AC wallbox (UL40/80A) <=1.8.32, 1.8.33
Terra AC wallbox (UL32A) <=1.8.2, 1.8.34
Terra AC wallbox (MID/ CE) <=1.8.32, 1.8.34
Terra AC wallbox (JP) <=1.8.2, 1.8.34





CVSS
Vendor
Equipment
Vulnerabilities




v3 6.8
ABB
ABB Terra AC
Heap-based Buffer Overflow




Background

Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Commercial Facilities, Critical Manufacturing, Energy, Transportation Systems
Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide
Company Headquarters Location: Switzerland


Vulnerabilities

Expand All +

CVE-2025-5517

There is potential risk to pollute the memory when a specially crafted OCPP message may be sent to a target vulnerable charger by exploiting unencrypted communication to the Charging Station Management System (CSMS) or fully remotely from its CSMS server.
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB Terra AC

Vendor:ABB
Product Version:ABB Terra AC wallbox (UL40/80A) <=1.8.32, ABB Terra AC wallbox (UL32A) <=1.8.2, ABB Terra AC wallbox (MID/ CE) Terra AC MID <=1.8.32, ABB Terra AC wallbox (JP) <=1.8.2
Product Status:fixed, known_affected


Remediations
Vendor fixThe problem is corrected in the product versions listed as fixed in the advisory. Terra AC wallbox (UL40/80A) 1.8.33 Terra AC wallbox (UL32A) 1.8.34 Terra AC MID 1.8.34 Terra AC Juno CE 1.8.34 Terra AC PTB 1.8.33 Terra AC wallbox (JP) 1.8.34 Additionally, we strongly recommend not use unsafe mode(http) to connect your charger to your backend even though OCPP is allowed to do in this way, which absolutely could be attacked by malicious man or organization as a common knowledge. ABB recommends that customers apply the update at earliest convenience.

Relevant CWE: CWE-122 Heap-based Buffer Overflow

Metrics




CVSS Version
Base Score
Base Severity
Vector String




3.1
6.8
MEDIUM
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H/E:P/RL:O/RC:C








Acknowledgments

Itai Shmueli of Saiflow reported this vulnerability to Schneider Electric.


Notice
The information in this document is subject to change without notice, and should not be construed as a commitment by ABB. ABB provides no warranty, express or implied, including warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, for the information contained in this document, and assumes no responsibility for any errors that may appear in this document. In no event shall ABB or any of its suppliers be liable for direct, indirect, special, incidental or consequential damages of any nature or kind arising from the use of this document, or from the use of any hardware or software described in this document, even if ABB or its suppliers have been advised of the possibility of such damages. This document and parts hereof must not be reproduced or copied without written permission from ABB, and the contents hereof must not be imparted to a third party nor used for any unauthorized purpose. All rights to registrations and trademarks reside with their respective owners.

Mitigating factors
To attack with this kind of message, hackers must hijack CSMS (OCPP backends) first and then can send messages, OR the way to OCPP backend is unsafe itself (http) which can cause any kind of attack behavior and known as a common knowledge. Refer to section “General security recommendations” for further advise on how to keep your system secure.

Workarounds
Make sure OCPP backend that chargers are connected is strictly secured to avoid any kind of at-tack especially the communication relevant components. Use https(TLS) as basic communication foundation between charger and OCPP backend instead of http.

Frequently asked questions
What causes the vulnerability? The vulnerability is caused by firmware which it didn’t limit the length of OCPP field in certain case. What is Terra AC wallbox? Terra AC wallbox is a Level 2 Electric Vehicle charger. What might an attacker use the vulnerability to do? An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could cause the affected system node to take control of the charger to response wrong messages, Denial-of-Service, compromised internal state, and possibly remote code execution. How could an attacker exploit the vulnerability? An attacker could try to exploit the vulnerability by sending a specially crafted OCPP message to chargers via OCPP backend(CSMS), which could be done remotely. This would require that the attacker has access to the system network and hijack the API of sending message OR hijack the network data directly if the charger is connected with unsafe http mode. Recommended practices help mitigate such attacks, see section Mitigating Factors above. Could the vulnerability be exploited remotely? Yes, an attacker who has network access to an affected system node could exploit this vulnerability. Can functional safety be affected by an exploit of this vulnerability? The charger potentially is running with unpredictable mode, including Denial-of-Service, compromised internal state, and possibly remote code execution. What does the update do? The update removes the vulnerability by modifying the validation rules of receiving data from OCPP backend. When this security advisory was issued, had this vulnerability been publicly disclosed? No, ABB received information about this vulnerability through responsible disclosure. When this security advisory was issued, had ABB received any reports that this vulnerability was being exploited? No, ABB had not received any information indicating that this vulnerability had been exploited when this security advisory was originally issued

This product is provided subject to this Notification (https://www.cisa.gov/notification) and this Privacy & Use policy (https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy).

Recommended Practices
CISA recommends users take defensive measures to minimize the exploitation risk of these vulnerabilities.
Minimize network exposure for all control system devices and/or systems, and ensure they are not accessible from the internet.
Locate control system networks and remote devices behind firewalls and isolate them from business networks.
When remote access is required, use more secure methods, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), recognizing VPNs may have vulnerabilities and should be updated to the most recent version available. Also recognize VPN is only as secure as its connected devices.
CISA reminds organizations to perform proper impact analysis and risk assessment prior to deploying defensive measures.
CISA also provides a section for control systems security recommended practices on the ICS webpage on cisa.gov. Several CISA products detailing cyber defense best practices are available for reading and download, including Improving Industrial Control Systems Cybersecurity with Defense-in-Depth Strategies.
CISA encourages organizations to implement recommended cybersecurity strategies for proactive defense of ICS assets. Additional mitigation guidance and recommended practices are publicly available on the ICS webpage at cisa.gov in the technical information paper, ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber Intrusion Detection and Mitigation Strategies.
Organizations observing suspected malicious activity should follow established internal procedures and report findings to CISA for tracking and correlation against other incidents.

CSAF 보기
요약
ABB는 권고에 영향을 받는 것으로 나열된 제품 버전의 취약점을 알고 있습니다. 이 취약점 악용에 성공한 공격자는 잠재적으로 제품을 원격으로 제어하고 플래시 메모리에 쓰기 작업을 수행하여 펌웨어 동작을 변경하는 힙 메모리 오염을 일으킬 수 있습니다.
영향을 받는 ABB Terra AC 버전은 다음과 같습니다.

Terra AC 월박스(UL40/80A) <=1.8.32, 1.8.33
Terra AC 월박스(UL32A) <=1.8.2, 1.8.34
Terra AC 월박스(MID/CE) <=1.8.32, 1.8.34
Terra AC 월박스(일본) <=1.8.2, 1.8.34





CVSS
공급업체
장비
취약점




v3 6.8
ABB
ABB 테라 AC
힙 기반 버퍼 오버플로




배경

중요 인프라 부문: 상업 시설, 중요 제조, 에너지, 운송 시스템
배포 국가/지역: 전 세계
회사 본사 위치: 스위스


취약점

모두 확장 +

CVE-2025-5517

CSMS(충전소 관리 시스템)에 대한 암호화되지 않은 통신을 이용하거나 CSMS 서버에서 완전히 원격으로 특수 제작된 OCPP 메시지가 취약한 대상 충전기에 전송될 수 있는 경우 메모리를 오염시킬 잠재적인 위험이 있습니다.
CVE 세부정보 보기

영향을 받는 제품
ABB 테라 AC

공급업체:ABB
제품 버전:ABB Terra AC wallbox(UL40/80A) <=1.8.32, ABB Terra AC wallbox(UL32A) <=1.8.2, ABB Terra AC wallbox(MID/CE) Terra AC MID <=1.8.32, ABB Terra AC wallbox(JP) <=1.8.2
제품 상태:수정됨, 알려진_영향을 받음


해결
공급업체 수정사항 권고에 수정된 것으로 나열된 제품 버전에서 문제가 수정되었습니다. Terra AC wallbox(UL40/80A) 1.8.33 Terra AC wallbox(UL32A) 1.8.34 Terra AC MID 1.8.34 Terra AC Juno CE 1.8.34 Terra AC PTB 1.8.33 Terra AC wallbox(JP) 1.8.34 또한 OCPP가 허용되더라도 충전기를 백엔드에 연결하는 데 안전하지 않은 모드(http)를 사용하지 않는 것이 좋습니다. 이렇게 하면 반드시 악의적인 사람이나 조직의 공격을 받을 수 있다는 것은 상식이다. ABB는 고객이 최대한 빠른 시일 내에 업데이트를 적용할 것을 권장합니다.

관련 CWE: CWE-122 힙 기반 버퍼 오버플로

측정항목




CVSS 버전
기본 점수
기본 심각도
벡터 문자열




3.1
6.8
중간
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H/E:P/RL:O/RC:C








감사의 말

Saiflow의 Itai Shmueli는 Schneider Electric에 이 취약점을 보고했습니다.


공지사항
본 문서의 정보는 예고 없이 변경될 수 있으며 ABB의 약속으로 해석되어서는 안 됩니다. ABB는 상품성 및 특정 목적에의 적합성에 대한 보증을 포함하여 본 문서에 포함된 정보에 대해 명시적이거나 묵시적인 어떠한 보증도 제공하지 않으며, 본 문서에 나타날 수 있는 오류에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. 어떠한 경우에도 ABB 또는 그 공급업체는 본 문서의 사용이나 본 문서에 설명된 하드웨어나 소프트웨어의 사용으로 인해 발생하는 모든 성격이나 종류의 직접적, 간접적, 특수적, 우발적 또는 파생적 손해에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. ABB 또는 그 공급업체가 그러한 손해의 가능성을 통보받은 경우에도 마찬가지입니다. 이 문서와 그 일부는 ABB의 서면 허가 없이 복제하거나 복사할 수 없으며, 이 문서의 내용을 제3자에게 전달하거나 승인되지 않은 목적으로 사용해서는 안 됩니다. 등록 및 상표에 대한 모든 권리는 해당 소유자에게 있습니다.

완화 요인
이런 종류의 메시지로 공격하려면 해커는 먼저 CSMS(OCPP 백엔드)를 하이재킹한 후 메시지를 보내야 합니다. 또는 OCPP 백엔드에 대한 경로 자체가 안전하지 않기 때문에(http) 모든 종류의 공격 동작이 발생할 수 있으며 상식으로 알려져 있습니다. 시스템 보안을 유지하는 방법에 대한 자세한 내용은 "일반 보안 권장 사항" 섹션을 참조하세요.

해결 방법
모든 종류의 공격, 특히 통신 관련 구성 요소를 방지하려면 충전기가 연결된 OCPP 백엔드가 엄격하게 보호되어 있는지 확인하세요. 충전기와 OCPP 백엔드 간의 기본 통신 기반으로 http 대신 https(TLS)를 사용합니다.

자주 묻는 질문
원인
vulnerability.summary_3lines:
1. 본 문서의 정보는 예고 없이 변경될 수 있으며 ABB의 약속으로 해석되어서는 안 됩니다.
2. 모든 종류의 공격, 특히 통신 관련 구성 요소를 방지하려면 충전기가 연결된 OCPP 백엔드
3. 심층 방어 전략을 통한 산업 제어 시스템 사이버 보안 개선을 포함하여 사이버 방어 모범 사

vulnerability.title: ABB Ability Zenon Remote Transport Vulnerability (Update A)
vulnerability.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
ABB is aware of vulnerabilities in the product versions listed as affected in the advisory. The vulnerability enables unauthorized access to the Reboot OS function within the Remote Transport Service, allowing an attacker to trigger a system reboot without the required authentication. This functionality initiates a system reboot on the target machine. However, remote exploitation of this vulnerability is not feasible unless the attacker has already gained access to the network where the affected ABB Ability Zenon system is deployed. At the time of writing, there is no evidence that this vulnerability is being actively exploited in the wild.
The following versions of ABB Ability Zenon Remote Transport Vulnerability are affected:

Ability Zenon >=7.50|<=14





CVSS
Vendor
Equipment
Vulnerabilities




v3 7.5
ABB
ABB Ability Zenon Remote Transport Vulnerability
Missing Authentication for Critical Function




Background

Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Chemical, Communications, Critical Manufacturing, Dams, Energy, Healthcare and Public Health, Information Technology, Water and Wastewater
Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide
Company Headquarters Location: Switzerland


Vulnerabilities

Expand All +

CVE-2025-8754

In the default configuration of the ABB Zenon software platform, the zensyssrv.exe service is set to start automatically. To utilize the Remote Transport Service, users are required to configure a password beforehand. However, a security vulnerability has been identified that enables unauthorized attackers to bypass authentication mechanisms and remotely initiate a system reboot without proper authorization
View CVE Details

Affected Products
ABB Ability Zenon Remote Transport Vulnerability

Vendor:ABB
Product Version:ABB Ability Zenon >=7.50|<=14
Product Status:known_affected


Remediations
Workaround• Restrict network access to systems with the ABB Zenon Software Platform installed. - Ensure that access to a system is restricted by implementing access controls to minimize the risk of unauthorized access. • Assess the necessity of the ABB Zenon Remote Transport functionality. - Ensure that if the Remote Transport functionality is not used, the zensyssrv.exe (ABB Zenon System Service) is stopped or terminated. The zensyssrv.exe can also be stopped or terminated after authorized use to prevent this vulnerability.

Relevant CWE: CWE-306 Missing Authentication for Critical Function

Metrics




CVSS Version
Base Score
Base Severity
Vector String




3.1
7.5
HIGH
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H








Acknowledgments

ABB PSIRT reported this vulnerability to CISA.


Notice
The information in this document is subject to change without notice, and should not be construed as a commitment by ABB. ABB provides no warranty, express or implied, including warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, for the information contained in this document, and assumes no responsibility for any errors that may appear in this document. In no event shall ABB or any of its suppliers be liable for direct, indirect, special, incidental or consequential damages of any nature or kind arising from the use of this document, or from the use of any hard-ware or software described in this document, even if ABB or its suppliers have been advised of the possibility of such damages. This document and parts hereof must not be reproduced or copied without written permission from ABB, and the contents hereof must not be imparted to a third party nor used for any unauthorized purpose. All rights to registrations and trademarks reside with their respective owners.

Work around
• Restrict network access to systems with the ABB zenon Software Platform installed. - Ensure that access to a system is restricted by implementing access controls to minimize the risk of unauthorized access. • Assess the necessity of the ABB Zenon Remote Transport functionality. - Ensure that if the Remote Transport functionality is not used, the zensyssrv.exe (ABB Zenon System Service) is stopped or terminated. The zensyssrv.exe can also be stopped or terminated after authorized use to prevent this vulnerability.

Legal Notice and Terms of Use
This product is provided subject to this Notification (https://www.cisa.gov/notification) and this Privacy & Use policy (https://www.cisa.gov/privacy-policy).

Recommended Practices
CISA recommends users take defensive measures to minimize the exploitation risk of these vulnerabilities.
Minimize network exposure for all control system devices and/or systems, and ensure they are not accessible from the internet.
Locate control system networks and remote devices behind firewalls and isolate them from business networks.
When remote access is required, use more secure methods, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), recognizing VPNs may have vulnerabilities and should be updated to the most recent version available. Also recognize VPN is only as secure as its connected devices.
CISA reminds organizations to perform proper impact analysis and risk assessment prior to deploying defensive measures.
CISA also provides a section for control systems security recommended practices on the ICS webpage on cisa.gov. Several CISA products detailing cyber defense best practices are available for reading and download, including Improving Industrial Control Systems Cybersecurity with Defense-in-Depth Strategies.
CISA encourages organizations to implement recommended cybersecurity strategies for proactive defense of ICS assets. Additional mitigation guidance and recommended practices are publicly available on the ICS webpage at cisa.gov in the technical information paper, ICS-TIP-12-146-01B--Targeted Cyber Intrusion Detection and Mitigation Strategies.
Organizations observing suspected malicious activity should follow established internal procedures and report findings to CISA for tracking and correlation against other incidents.

Advisory Conversion Disclaimer
This ICSA is a verbatim republication of ABB PSIRT 2NGA002743 from a direct conversion of the vendor's Common Security Advisory Framework (CSAF) advisory. This is republished to CISA's website as a means of increasing visibility and is provided "as-is" for informational purposes only. CISA is not responsible for the editorial or technical accuracy of republished advisories and provides no warranties of any kind regarding any information contained within this advisory. Further, CISA does not endorse any commercial product or service. Please contact ABB PSIRT directly for any questions regarding this advisory.

vulnerability.title_ko: ABB Ability Zenon 원격 전송 취약점(업데이트 A)
vulnerability.summary_ko:
CSAF 보기
요약
ABB는 권고에 영향을 받는 것으로 나열된 제품 버전의 취약점을 알고 있습니다. 이 취약점으로 인해 원격 전송 서비스 내의 OS 재부팅 기능에 대한 무단 액세스가 가능해 공격자가 필요한 인증 없이 시스템 재부팅을 실행할 수 있습니다. 이 기능은 대상 시스템에서 시스템 재부팅을 시작합니다. 그러나 공격자가 영향을 받는 ABB Ability Zenon 시스템이 배포된 네트워크에 이미 액세스 권한을 얻지 않은 한 이 취약점을 원격으로 악용하는 것은 불가능합니다. 이 글을 쓰는 시점에는 이 취약점이 실제로 활발하게 악용되고 있다는 증거가 없습니다.
ABB Ability Zenon 원격 전송 취약점의 다음 버전이 영향을 받습니다.

능력 제논 >=7.50|<=14





CVSS
공급업체
장비
취약점




v3 7.5
ABB
ABB 능력 Zenon 원격 전송 취약점
중요한 기능에 대한 인증 누락




배경

중요 인프라 부문: 화학, 통신, 중요 제조, 댐, 에너지, 의료 및 공중 보건, 정보 기술, 수자원 및 폐수
배포 국가/지역: 전 세계
회사 본사 위치: 스위스


취약점

모두 확장 +

CVE-2025-8754

ABB Zenon 소프트웨어 플랫폼의 기본 구성에서는 zensyssrv.exe 서비스가 자동으로 시작되도록 설정되어 있습니다. 원격운송 서비스를 이용하시려면 사전에 비밀번호를 설정하셔야 합니다. 그러나 무단 공격자가 인증 메커니즘을 우회하고 적절한 승인 없이 원격으로 시스템 재부팅을 시작할 수 있는 보안 취약점이 확인되었습니다.
CVE 세부정보 보기

영향을 받는 제품
ABB 능력 Zenon 원격 전송 취약점

공급업체:ABB
제품 버전:ABB Ability Zenon >=7.50|<=14
제품 상태:알려짐_영향을 받음


해결
해결 방법 • ABB Zenon 소프트웨어 플랫폼이 설치된 시스템에 대한 네트워크 액세스를 제한합니다. - 무단 액세스 위험을 최소화하기 위해 액세스 제어를 구현하여 시스템에 대한 액세스를 제한합니다. • ABB Zenon 원격 전송 기능의 필요성을 평가합니다. - 원격 전송 기능을 사용하지 않는 경우 zensyssrv.exe(ABB Zenon System Service)가 중지되거나 종료되는지 확인하십시오. 이 취약점을 방지하기 위해 승인된 사용 후에 zensyssrv.exe를 중지하거나 종료할 수도 있습니다.

관련 CWE: CWE-306 중요한 기능에 대한 인증 누락

측정항목




CVSS 버전
기본 점수
기본 심각도
벡터 문자열




3.1
7.5
높음
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H








감사의 말

ABB PSIRT는 이 취약점을 CISA에 보고했습니다.


공지사항
본 문서의 정보는 예고 없이 변경될 수 있으며 ABB의 약속으로 해석되어서는 안 됩니다. ABB는 상품성 및 특정 목적에의 적합성에 대한 보증을 포함하여 본 문서에 포함된 정보에 대해 명시적이거나 묵시적인 어떠한 보증도 제공하지 않으며, 본 문서에 나타날 수 있는 오류에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. 어떠한 경우에도 ABB 또는 그 공급업체는 본 문서의 사용이나 본 문서에 설명된 하드웨어나 소프트웨어의 사용으로 인해 발생하는 모든 성격이나 종류의 직접적, 간접적, 특수적, 우발적 또는 파생적 손해에 대해 책임을 지지 않습니다. 이는 ABB 또는 그 공급업체가 그러한 손해의 가능성을 통보받은 경우에도 마찬가지입니다. 이 문서와 그 일부는 ABB의 서면 허가 없이 복제하거나 복사할 수 없으며, 이 문서의 내용을 제3자에게 전달하거나 승인되지 않은 목적으로 사용해서는 안 됩니다. 등록 및 상표에 대한 모든 권리는 해당 소유자에게 있습니다.

해결 방법
• ABB zenon 소프트웨어 플랫폼이 설치된 시스템에 대한 네트워크 액세스를 제한합니다. - 무단 액세스 위험을 최소화하기 위해 액세스 제어를 구현하여 시스템에 대한 액세스를 제한합니다. • ABB Zenon 원격 전송 기능의 필요성을 평가합니다. - 원격 전송 기능이 켜져 있는지 확인하십시오.
